# Contributor License Agreement (CLA)

<!-- TODO: Have the agreements (ICLA.md / ECLA.md) reviewed by a lawyer before accepting any external contribution. -->
<!-- TODO: Set up CLA Assistant (https://cla-assistant.io) and fill the signing URL in "the instructions at ___" placeholder inside ICLA.md / ECLA.md. -->

Thank you for your interest in contributing to **SculptOps** ("the Project"), maintained by **Refacto** ("We", "Us").

To keep the Project sustainable, every contributor must agree to a Contributor License Agreement before their contribution can be merged. This page explains why, and points you to the right agreement to sign.

---

## Why a CLA?

SculptOps is **open source under AGPL-3.0**, and that does not change. The CLA exists so that Refacto holds the rights needed to:

- keep distributing the Project under AGPL-3.0 to everyone, **and**
- offer a commercial/proprietary license for organizations that cannot comply with the AGPL (dual-licensing).

The CLA grants Refacto an **exclusive license** over contributions, with a non-exclusive license back to you for your own contribution (see the agreement for details).

The publicly available code stays AGPL-3.0 regardless. The CLA only governs **Refacto's** rights, not the public license you receive.

---

## Which agreement do I sign?

| You are… | Sign |
|---|---|
| An individual contributing on your own behalf | **[ICLA.md](./ICLA.md)** — Individual Contributor License Agreement |
| Contributing as part of your employment, or on behalf of a company | **[ECLA.md](./ECLA.md)** — Entity Contributor License Agreement (your employer must approve) |

Both are governed by the laws of **France** and include a Traditional Patent License grant.

---

## How to sign

When you open a Pull Request, our CLA bot checks whether you have signed. If not, it posts a comment with a link to sign via GitHub OAuth. **Your PR cannot be merged until the CLA is signed.**

<!-- TODO: Replace with the actual CLA Assistant sign-in URL once configured. -->

Questions? Contact us at **contact@sculptops.io**.
